title: Viguiera oblonga
intro: Viguiera oblonga, also known as "tree daisy" or "tree marigold," is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its showy yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with its leaves and flowers exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, colds, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Viguiera oblonga include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made with Viguiera oblonga.
taxonomy: Viguiera oblonga is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant belongs to the tribe Heliantheae, which includes a large number of flowering plants. The genus Viguiera is a group of flowering plants native to North America, and Viguiera oblonga is one of its species.
